entry.title: Re: When to create my own badi?
entry.content: The title of your question was clear, but then you have lost yourself in unrelated explanations. Clear questions = clear and fast answers. If you want your fellow developers in your company to implement code at given "exit" positions in the future, you define an enhancement option (i.e. CALL BADI) like SAP does for the customers. If you're in a small project with very few people and very little custom code, there is no big reason to define enhancement options.

entry.title: Re: Restricting POD page access on Badge ID (Two differen...
entry.content:
Enabling Badge Access on POD pages is designed for shared terminal or shared user environments, allowing the system to accurately track which specific user (via badge) performed each specific action in that POD page. It does not provide any additional levels of authorization or access control by role, it’s meant for user identification and traceability.

Learn more at https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/pod-designer/adding-pages-to-pod and Enable faster user changes and shared terminals using badges in SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: In SAP DM, Production orders listed in post productio...
entry.content:
By design, Post-Production Reporting POD is meant to work with production/process orders executed in Order POD flow with Autocomplete and Confirm flag as NO and Relaxed Flow flag as YES in the routing/recipe, not in Work Center POD flow, which has different requirements.

Hope it clarifies,
Manoel

entry.title: CDS: How to use @AnalyticsDetails.elimination annotation to eliminate the internal business volume
entry.content:
This blog is about how to use @AnalyticsDetails.elimination to eliminate the internal business volume when executing an analytical query.

Internal business volume

When revenues made between two subsidiaries of company, for company’s total revenue, these revenues should not be considered, as transaction is happening within the company. For example, a company is divided into several subsidiaries. These subsidiaries are divided into further subsidiaries. This means there exists a hierarchy of subsidiaries. Subsidiaries are selling products to each other. For each subsidiary this is external sales. But for a parent subsidiary this is just internal sales, as both subsidiaries are descendants. By using this annotation, revenues made between two subsidiaries in an organization are no longer displayed.

More information about the concept can be found Elimination of Internal Business Volume | SAP Help Portal

Syntax

elimination: {

     pair : array of {

       dimension1: String(30);

       dimension2: String(30);

     }

     rule : String(20) enum { ELIMINATE_IF_ALL; ELIMINATE_IF_ONE; };

   }

The annotation can be used in cube-views (Analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE) or in analytical queries (define transient view entity with provider contract analytical query).

  • pair: this list defines the pairs (dimension1 and dimension2) which are checked for elimination. Both dimensions have needed a foreign key association to the same target view. If the annotation is used on cube level, both dimensions must be elements of the cube. If the annotation is used in an analytical query, both dimensions must be part of the source cube view.
  • rule: if set to #ELIMINATE_IF_ALL, then the volume is eliminated if all pairs are internal. If set to #ELIMINATE_IF_ONE the volume is eliminated if at least one pair is internal. If "rule" is not set, the AND-logic (ELIMINATE_IF_ALL) is applied.

Example 

Following example displays the number of seat booked in flight according to the elimination rule specified on destination airport, departure airport, destination country, departure country.

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'Elimination of Internal Busniess volumn'
@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_QUERY
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [#ANALYTICAL_QUERY]
define transient view entity ZLR_PC_FLIGHTICE_SIMPLE
  provider contract analytical_query
  as projection on ZOQ_FLIGHT_ICE
{

  _airportfrom._hier( p_HierarchyID : 'GEO' ) as _airportFromHier,
  @Consumption.hidden: true
  _airportfrom.AirportID                      as dummyAirportFrom,


  _airportto._hier( p_HierarchyID : 'GEO' )   as _airportToHier,
  @Consumption.hidden: true
  _airportto.AirportID                        as dummyAirportTo,
  
  @AnalyticsDetails.query: {
    axis: #ROWS,
    displayHierarchy: #ON,
    hierarchyAssociation: '_airportFromHier'
  }
  
  airportfrom,
  
  @AnalyticsDetails.query: {
    axis: #ROWS,
    displayHierarchy: #ON,
    hierarchyAssociation: '_airportToHier'
  }
  airportto,

  @Consumption.filter.defaultValue: 'DE'
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
  countryfrom,

  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
  countryto,

  seatsmax,



  @AnalyticsDetails.elimination: {
    pair: [{ dimension1 : 'countryfrom' , dimension2: 'countryto' }]
  }
  @EndUserText.label: 'SeatsMax Q-ICE country'
  seatsmax                                    as seatsmaxICEQ_C,


  @AnalyticsDetails.elimination:{
    pair: [ { dimension1 : 'airportfrom' , dimension2: 'airportto' },
            { dimension1 : 'countryfrom' , dimension2: 'countryto' } ],
    rule: #ELIMINATE_IF_ALL
  }
  @EndUserText.label: 'SeatsMax Q-ICE airport and country'
  seatsmax                                    as seatsmaxICEQ_AC,


  @AnalyticsDetails.elimination:{
    pair: [ { dimension1 : 'airportfrom' , dimension2: 'airportto' },
            { dimension1 : 'countryfrom' , dimension2: 'countryto' } ],
    rule: #ELIMINATE_IF_ONE
  }
  @EndUserText.label: 'SeatsMax Q-ICE airport or country'
  seatsmax                                    as seatsmaxICEQ_A_C


}

 

Output

 

In this example for columns (cells marked with colour): 

SeatsMax Q-ICE country – elimination pair mentioned as 'countryfrom' and 'countryto'. Therefore, there is no entry where departure country and destination country are same.

SeatsMax Q-ICE airport and country- elimination pairs are 'airportfrom' and 'airportto', 'countryfrom' and 'countryto' And rule as #ELIMINATE_IF_ALL, Therefore, there is no entry where destination airport and departure airport are same AND destination country and departure country are same.

SeatsMax Q-ICE airport or country - elimination pairs are 'airportfrom' and 'airportto', 'countryfrom' and 'countryto' And rule as #ELIMINATE_IF_ONE, Therefore, there is no entry where destination airport and departure airport are same OR destination country and departure country are same.

 

Constraints

The annotation can be used in cubes or in queries.

  • The dimension names in one pair (AnalyticsDetails.elimination.pair) must have a foreign key association to the same dimension view and this dimension view must have an hierarchy association.
  • In the query definition:
    • It is not necessary that any of the dimensions in the list of pairs is part of the query
    • If a hierarchy is assigned to a dimension, then the pair dimension must be assigned to the same hierarchy or must be without hierarchy
    • If a dimension is not in drill down, then the condition of the one pair is always true (independent of the value of the other dimension of the pair). The same is true on totals/subtotal lines.

entry.title: Re: Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi,

There is very limited information available, but I have the feeling that a planned batch might be missing in your order header, which is normally generated in S/4 and sent to DM via Order Download, and used during Goods Receipt posting.

Double check and make sure a planned batch is defined in Goods Receipt tab of your order in ERP and shows up in Manage Orders app header at 'batch' field and try again.

Otherwise, you might need to raise a ticket for further investigation and proper support.

Hope it helps!
Manoel

entry.title: Introducing the Canada ERP Localization Work Zone
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the launch of our brand-new Work Zone dedicated to Canada ERP Localization. This dedicated space aims to bring together relevant information and resources, making it easier for our customers to find what they need.

What to Expect in the Work Zone

  • Centralized Content Repository

Our primary objective is to offer a centralized, comprehensive resource hub for Canada ERP Localization. In our content repository, you'll discover essential files, including those pertaining to Canada DRC reports.

  • Active Engagement through the Forum

One of our key objectives is to foster a collaborative environment where we can hear directly from our customers. We have introduced a "Forum" section where customers can actively participate. Our first discussion topic is: "Which content would you like to see added to this Work Zone?" We are eager to hear your responses so that we can continue building this Work Zone with valuable insights that benefit your businesses.

  • Related Links and FAQs

We have also included a section with related links and frequently asked questions (FAQs). This will help you quickly find answers to common queries and access additional resources.

How to Join the Work Zone

To request access to this private Work Zone Group, please follow these steps:

  1. Open the Workspace link: Canada — Enterprise Resource Planning Localization
  2. Click the "Request to Join" button.
  3. Use your work email to submit the request.
  4. Include your company name, your full name, and your role in the comment section.

Existing Support Procedure for SAP Products Remains in Place

Please be reminded that if you experience any difficulties or need assistance with an SAP product, you should continue to follow the standard procedure of creating an incident via ServiceNow.

Join the Conversation

We look forward to your active participation in our Work Zone. Together, we can build a robust and valuable resource that meets the needs of our Canadian customers.

Access the Work Zone here: Canada — Enterprise Resource Planning Localization

Together, let's make the Canada ERP Localization Work Zone a vibrant hub of information and collaboration.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: How to get material group description, not its key, i...
entry.content:
Hi there,

You can use the "I_ProductGroupText_2" datasource, it has ProductGroupText name field, which is the Product Group Description.

PS.: Product and material are basically the same thing.

You can retrieve this data with "API_PRODUCTGROUP_2" too! Its documentation is available at Product Group Data - Read | Business Accelerator Hub

Hope this helps you out!

Kind regards,
Ícaro
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA BP screen enhancement with custom tab
entry.content:
Hi Andi,

Is it possible to delete the '3050099' entry from BUS6?

If I try to delete it, it gives the following message:

Nr. mesaj SV117

Diagnosis

You have changed an entry which, according to the name space definition is managed by SAP.

System Response

Your changes may be overwritten at each subsequent upgrade or release change.

Procedure

Avoid changing this data. If the change is inavoidable, document it carefully, so that it can be repeated, if necessary, after an upgrade or release change. The best way to do this is to put the data in a change request, which you can export before an upgrade or release change, and import again afterwards.

entry.title: SAP Asset Performance Management Embedded IoT: Device Management is available via EAC
entry.content:
As you might recall, Embedded IoT has been generally available as part of SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) since last year.   With the latest release, 2505, we are bringing more exciting Embedded IoT capabilities around Device Management to you via Early Adopter Care (EAC) and enable more Cumulocity features inside of APM:

1. Directly connecting to smart devices and gateways to APM is now faster and easier :

  • Plug-and-play integration with Cumulocity-certified devices.
  • Native support for MQTT, REST, OPC UA, and fieldbus protocols like CAN, Profibus, and Modbus.
  • Built-in device management features including over-the-air updates, remote diagnostics, and configuration management. secure onboarding.

This helps organizations tackle the challenges of fragmented IoT landscapes and scale their connected asset ecosystem with reduced risk and cost.

2. A user-friendly experience for reliability engineers to interact and visualize with IoT-enabled assets :

  • Personalized, no-code/low-code dashboards and reports.
  • Interact directly with assets for remote operations.
  • Ready-to-use widget collections for typical IoT use-cases.

With this unified view, Cockpit, reliability engineers can act faster and make smarter decisions without jumping between tools.

3. Contextualize the IoT data bridge between the IoT/OT with the SAP S/4HANA asset data model:

  • Model IoT devices in Cumulocity and technical objects in SAP Cloud ERP.
  • Map data points (sensors/tags) to APM indicators.

Lower integration costs with a prepackaged, GUI-driven experience called Digital Twin Manager to empower data ops and reliability engineers.  This feature will also be further enhanced in an upcoming release.

 

To take advantage of these new innovations for testing and production, please raise a ticket to request early access.  With closer collaboration with the product engineering team, EAC enables two-way communication, transparency, and allows stakeholders to minimize project risk.

 

 

I’d also like to give you a quick preview of what’s to come on the product roadmap:

4. Real-time asset condition monitoring

  • Reliability engineers can define streaming rules using a GUI without relying on IT or data scientists.
  • The system continuously processes live data to detect conditions, calculate KPIs and trigger alerts, emails and event IoT response.

This Streaming Analytics capability enables a shift from reactive to proactive maintenance with minimal latency and dramatically improved reaction time in case of critical asset conditions.  Please stay tuned for more on this upcoming capability in the community.

 

Finally, please check out this Interactive Journey to get a glimpse of the end-to-end experience would look like: https://ivj-vx.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/journey/c372d1a6-8e76-4adb-82da-c014881ba331

entry.title: Re: How to clear parameter after clicking selective radio...
entry.content:
Hi @JayeshHati1997,

You're on the right path! Getting stuck is part of the process, and it's great that you're practicing and doing your best to solve things on your own. Keep at it!

Regarding your code:

You're using AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT with a LOOP AT SCREEN, trying to clear fields based on radiobutton selection. However, this doesn't work as expected because:

  1. AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT is executed before the screen is displayed and there are other events that are more suitable for this handling.

  2.  screen-name CS 'pradius' only checks the name of the screen field, not whether the user selected a radiobutton. To check if a radiobutton is selected, you should use a direct check like:

IF p_rad EQ 'X'.
  " Your logic here
ENDIF.

  That means p_rad is selected. If it’s initial, it means it's not selected. The same applies to p_dia.

Recommended fix:

    Use the USER-COMMAND rb that you added to the radio button group and handle the logic in the AT SELECTION-SCREEN event. This way, the code runs after the user interacts with the radiobuttons in the GRB1 group:

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF sy-ucomm EQ 'RB'.
    " Your logic here.
  ENDIF.

You can also use:

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON RADIOBUTTON GROUP grb1.

 

With the event AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON RADIOBUTTON GROUP grb1 you don't need the USER-COMMAND rb defined in the PARAMETER and can handle it from the identifier of the radibutton group.

For further reference and more examples, these SAP documentation links might help:
  • Selection Screens – Block and Radio Button Processing ( Good code examples for your request );
  • AT SELECTION-SCREEN statement.

Hope this helps. Keep experimenting!
Any questions, just let me know.

Regards,
Hugo Loiola.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - Update downtime timestamp with downtime API
entry.content:
Hi,

To address your requirement, I suggest you use /resource/v2/changestatus instead, to change the resource status to UNSCHEDULED_DOWN, for example, once the downtime starts:

And change the resource status back to PRODUCTIVE, once it ends:

Which should create the downtimes for you automatically:

Check /resource/v2/changestatus for more details.

Hope it helps! Br,
Manoel

entry.title: How to inspect memory usage in Cloud Integration?
entry.content:
Hi community,

According to the document, we can inspect system memory usage via the Inspect > Memory app. 

In the application, "Maximum Message Size" is shown in the right axis. This number indicates the messeage size, and not memory consumption over a given time period. Although Memory Exhaustions are displayed as a red line, we have no way to check the actual memory consumption unless memory is exhausted. 

Is there any way we can check the actual memory consumption in Cloud Integration?

entry.title: The requested lookup key was not found in any active activation context
entry.content:
hi all, When I import addon into sap for startup, it prompts me that the requested lookup key was not found in any active activation context.
My sap b1 version number is 10.00.250, .net framework4.8
So I want to know, is this problem a problem with my addon code or Settings, or is it a Windows problem? I have checked relevant materials and some say it's a problem caused by Windows.
The following is my code. Please help analyze it
class Program

  class Program
    {
        public static SAPbouiCOM.Application SBO_Application;
        public static SAPbobsCOM.Company oCompany;
        public static string SelectedItemCode = ""; 
        /// <summary>
        /// The main entry point for the application.
        /// </summary>
        [STAThread]
        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            try
            {
                SAPbouiCOM.SboGuiApi sboGuiApi = new SAPbouiCOM.SboGuiApi();
                string sConnectionString = args.Length > 0 ? args[0] : Environment.GetCommandLineArgs()[1];
                sboGuiApi.Connect(sConnectionString);
                SBO_Application = sboGuiApi.GetApplication(-1);             
                oCompany = (SAPbobsCOM.Company)SBO_Application.Company.GetDICompany();
                if (oCompany.Connected)
                {
                    SBO_Application.StatusBar.SetText("Connection successful! DI Company is ready", SAPbouiCOM.BoMessageTime.bmt_Short, SAPbouiCOM.BoStatusBarMessageType.smt_Success);
                }
                else
                {
                    SBO_Application.StatusBar.SetText("Connection failed. Please check the connection information", SAPbouiCOM.BoMessageTime.bmt_Short, SAPbouiCOM.BoStatusBarMessageType.smt_Error);
                    return;
                }
                Application oApp = null;
                if (args.Length < 1)
                {
                    oApp = new Application();
                }
                else
                {
                    //If you want to use an add-on identifier for the development license, you can specify an add-on identifier string as the second parameter.
                    //oApp = new Application(args[0], "XXXXX");
                    oApp = new Application(args[0]);
                }
                Menu MyMenu = new Menu();               
                MyMenu.AddMenuItems();
                oApp.RegisterMenuEventHandler(MyMenu.SBO_Application_MenuEvent);
                Application.SBO_Application.AppEvent += new SAPbouiCOM._IApplicationEvents_AppEventEventHandler(SBO_Application_AppEvent);
                setAttribute setA = new setAttribute(SBO_Application, oCompany);
                setA.Initialize();
                oApp.Run();
                
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                System.Windows.Forms.MessageBox.Show(ex.Message);
            }
        }

        

        static void SBO_Application_AppEvent(SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes EventType)
        {
            switch (EventType)
            {
                case SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes.aet_ShutDown:
                    //Exit Add-On
                    System.Windows.Forms.Application.Exit();
                    break;
                case SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes.aet_CompanyChanged:
                    break;
                case SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes.aet_FontChanged:
                    break;
                case SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes.aet_LanguageChanged:
                    break;
                case SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes.aet_ServerTerminition:
                    break;
                default:
                    break;
            }
        }
    }

  class setAttribute

 

 

private Application SBO_Application;
        private SAPbobsCOM.Company oCompany; 
        string openProDocentry = "";
        public setAttribute(SAPbouiCOM.Application app, SAPbobsCOM.Company company)
        {
            this.SBO_Application = app;
            this.oCompany = company;
        }
        public void Initialize()
        {            
            SBO_Application.AppEvent += new _IApplicationEvents_AppEventEventHandler(SBO_Application_AppEvent);
            SBO_Application.ItemEvent += new _IApplicationEvents_ItemEventEventHandler(SBO_Application_ItemEvent);
            SBO_Application.MenuEvent += new _IApplicationEvents_MenuEventEventHandler(SBO_Application_MenuEvent);
            SBO_Application.MessageBox("Event registration completed and connection successful!");
        }

When I activate addon, it will prompt "Connection successful!" DI Company is ready

entry.title: Re: Regarding of extensibility of [Open AP/AR Items] of a...
entry.content:
Hello @yukitsumura 

Thank you for your question.

After talking to the experts, I can provide the following answer: It's currently not supported to display custom fields in the Open AP/AR Items table in the Clear Incoming Payments app. 

You may request features on the Customer Influence Portal . We continuously review and prioritize feature requests based on customer feedback. 

Kind regards,

Anne

entry.title: Re: Confirmation of production order through BAPI
entry.content:
Hello

This is not possible due to the BAPI design (goods movements are posted asynchronously, in the update task). 

If you need to have the goods receipt posted to continue with any subsequent process, you might post it before the confirmation, using the MM BAPI.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Sunset of BEx Tools - Frequently Asked Questions and Answers (FAQ)
entry.content:
Sunset of BEx Tools - Frequently Asked Questions and Answers (FAQs):

With support for BEx Analyzer, Workbooks, and Broadcaster ending in October 2025, organizations using SAP BW need to act now. This article answers the most frequently asked questions around the BEx tool sunset, outlines key dates, risks, and support limitations, and explores recommended replacement tools like SAP Analysis for Office and SAP Analytics Cloud

We have grouped the questions by components to make the information clear, simple, and component-specific.

  • General Questions about End of Support
  • Migration and Replacement Tools
  • Technical and Operational Concerns
  • Conversion Questions
  • License
  • Planning and Next Steps

For an Overview of the BEx tools sunset including the rationale, timelines, and key actions customers should consider ahead of October 2025, refer to previous detailed blog post:
👉Sunset of BEx Tools in SAP BW – What You Need to Know Before October 2025 This foundational article complements the FAQs by offering strategic guidance for planning your transition effectively.

 

General Questions about End of Support:

What specific BEx tools are affected by the end of support in October 2025?

  • BEx tools dependent on excel, such as BEx Analyzer, BEx Workbooks, and BEx Broadcaster (Pre-calculation server) are impacted

What are the key dates we need to be aware of for the end of support?

  • October 14, 2025: End of Microsoft support for Office 2016 and 2019, impacting BEx Analyzer / Workbook and BEx Broadcaster (Pre-calculation server)
  • December 31, 2027: End of mainstream maintenance for SAP BW 7.5.
  • December 31, 2030: End of extended maintenance for SAP BW 7.5 and BI Java based Broadcasting and BEx Query Designer
  • Note: Users on older SAP BW versions (e.g., 7.4 or earlier) have already past their mainstream maintenance end dates in 2020.

What happens if we continue to use BEx Excel dependent tools after the end of support dates?

  • You may face several risks, including:
    • No SAP Support: SAP will no longer provide patches, bug fixes, or technical assistance for issues related to impacted BEx tools. Incident support will not be available in case of any issues faced.
    • Compatibility Problems: Future updates to operating systems, Office versions, SAP GUI Patches etc. may break BEx functionality.
    • Security and Compliance Risks: Unsupported software is more vulnerable to security threats. Without security updates from Microsoft or SAP, any vulnerabilities discovered after the end-of-support date will remain unpatched, increasing the risk of data breaches or compliance violations
    • No Future Enhancements: There will be no new features or improvements for BEx Excel-dependent tools. Any issues with integration, performance, or usability will need to be managed internally, without help from SAP

Is there any way to get support for BEx Excel dependent tools after the October 14’ 2025?

  • No. There is no Extended maintenance or Customer specific maintenance available for BEx Analyzer and BEx Broadcaster support beyond October 2025, even at additional cost.

What about embedded BW systems (e.g., ECC, SCM, CRM) using BEx tools, are they also impacted?

  • Yes. Any system with the SAP_BW component, including embedded BW in SAP ERP systems, is impacted if excel based BEx tools are used for reporting.

Migration and Replacement Tools:

What are the recommended replacement tools for BEx Analyzer and BEx Workbooks?

  • SAP recommends transitioning to:
    • SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (AO) for Excel-based reporting with similar layout and filtering capabilities. SAP offers Mass Conversion Tool through SAP Services to convert existing BEx Workbooks to Analysis for Office workbooks. Manual conversion of BEx to AO workbook also possible.
    • SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Excel Add-in for modern, web-based dashboards, stories, and advanced analytics. There is No automated conversion tool exist for converting BEx Workbooks directly to SAC Excel Add-in. Migration requires manual rebuilding of reports and dashboards in SAC

What are the recommended replacement tools for BEx Broadcasting (Pre-calculation server)?

  • Broadcasting / Scheduling in SAP BW systems is used to schedule and distribute BEx Query and Workbook outputs via email, portal, or printer, leveraging different SAP broadcasting solutions such as Pre-calculation Server, BI Java Scheduling, SAC Publication, Analysis for office scheduling
  • BEx Broadcaster (Pre-calculation Server) support will end in October 2025, aligning with the broader SAP BEx sunset
  • Broadcasting via BI Java scheduling is available but not considered a long-term strategic option due to planned EOMM by 2027 and EOEM by 2030
  • Recommended Alternatives for scheduling and distribution:
    • SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence: Offers built-in scheduling capabilities through the SAP BusinessObjects platform, including the ability to schedule Analysis for Office Workbooks
    • SAP Analytics Cloud Publication: Strategic SAP solution for modern, cloud-based report distribution and scheduling

What are the recommended replacement tools for BEx Web Application Designer (WAD)?

  • BEx Web Application Designer is supported with BW on HANA and BW/4HANA until 2030, subject to the end of mainstream maintenance in 2027 and the availability of extended maintenance through 2030.
  • SAP recommends replacing BEx Web with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) as primary replacement tool

Is there any impact on BI Java or BOBJ based Broadcasting tools with this BEx tool sunset?

  • No. there is no impact to BI Java or BOBJ based broadcasting tools.
  • However, BI Java based broadcasting is supported as per the maintenance timelines for BW 7.50 i.e. until 2027 (mainstream maintenance) and until 2030 (Extended Maintenance) with BW 7.5 and BW/4HANA.

What are the migration paths for our existing BEx workbooks to Analysis for Office?

  • For customers with a large number of BEx Workbooks, we recommend engaging with SAP MaxAttention or the SAP Services team, which offers a mass conversion tool to automate the migration of BEx Workbooks to Analysis for Office format
  • For customers with only a limited number of BEx Workbooks, a manual migration  using Analysis for Office may be more practical

What are the migration paths for our existing BEx workbooks to SAC?

  • There is currently no automated conversion tool available to migrate BEx Workbooks directly to SAC
  • As a result, any migration from BEx Workbooks to SAC requires a manual rebuild of the report content using SAC’s capabilities

What are the migration paths for our existing BEx Broadcasting to SAC Publication?

  • There is no direct migration path or automated tool to convert existing BEx Broadcaster (Pre-calculation server) to SAC Publications
  • Customers must manually recreate broadcasting logic using SAC’s Publication and Scheduling features

What resources or support does SAP offer for this migration?

  • For customers with a large volume of BEx Workbooks, SAP MaxAttention or SAP Services team provide:
    • A mass conversion tool developed internally by SAP Services to automate workbook migration
    • Expert-led migration assessments, scoping, and planning workshops
    • Support for functional validation and post-migration support

Technical and Operational Concerns:

Will our existing SAP BW system (e.g., BW 7.5) still be compatible with BEx tools if we don't upgrade Office?

  • Technically, your SAP BW 7.5 system will continue to support the excel independent BEx tools (BEx Query Designer) until the end of its own maintenance (mainstream until December 31, 2027, and extended until 2030).
  • However. excel dependent tools (BEx Analyzer/Workbooks, BEx Broadcasting – Pre-calculation server) will have end of support by October 2025.

If we are planning to move to SAP BW/4HANA, what is the role of BEx tools in that landscape?

  • BEx tools are not supported with BW/4HANA and already has replacement path
    • BEx Analyzer / Workbooks à Analysis for Office (Conversion)
    • BEx Broadcasting (Pre-calculation server) àBI Java Broadcasting / BOBJ scheduling / SAC Publication (Rebuild)
    • BEx Queries à Queries (No changes)
    • BEx Query Designer à Eclipsed based BW-MT Modeling tool (Modeling tool change)

If customers continue using BEx Analyzer / Workbooks / BEx Broadcaster with Office 365 or newer platforms, and something breaks, will SAP support incidents still be accepted?

  • No. If issues arise due to platform incompatibility, SAP support incidents will not be accepted or resolved for these legacy tools.
  • Customers are strongly encouraged to migrate to SAP Analysis for Office and SAP Analytics Cloud, which are actively supported and compatible with modern office environments.

Since BEx Analyzer is tightly coupled with SAP GUI for Windows, will SAP continue to deliver SAP GUI updates (compatible with BEx) for the full NetWeaver 7.5 maintenance window (i.e., through end of 2027 or 2030 extended)?

  • No GUI updates will be delivered related to obsolete BEx tools (BEx Analyzer / Workbooks and BEx Broadcasting (Pre-calculation server)

Are there any planned deprecations or changes in SAP GUI that could break BEx Analyzer compatibility before NetWeaver 7.5 is retired?

  • Yes, there are planned deprecations and technical shifts in SAP GUI and the Microsoft Office ecosystem that could affect BEx Analyzer compatibility, even before SAP NetWeaver 7.5 reaches end of mainstream maintenance in December 2027

Will our existing BEx Queries still work with the BEx Query designer tools?

  • You can still design and maintain queries using BEx Query Designer until the end of 2030
  • If you are running BW 7.5 on HANA or BW/4HANA, you also have the option to use the Eclipse-based BW Modeling Tools (BW-MT) for query design and modeling

Will my existing BEx Workbooks continue to work after Office 2016/2019 support ends?

  • Technically, BEx Workbooks may work after October 14’ 2025, however there will no SAP support for issues
  • Because Microsoft and SAP will stop providing security updates, any new vulnerabilities found after this date will remain unpatched, increasing the risk of security breaches
  • BEx tools are delivered as Frontend Patches (BEx FEPs) built specifically for Office 2016/2019, including signed files (.xla, .exe). After support ends, SAP will no longer release these installation files for Excel-dependent BEx tools. This means you can only keep using BEx Analyzer and Workbooks if you stay on a previously released BEx FEP version and keep Excel 2016 or 2019 installed. Even then, SAP support will be very limited, only addressing known issues fixed in earlier patches or backend problems, with no new bug fixes for the frontend
  • In addition, there is a potential risk that Microsoft’s compatibility between Office 365 and Office 2016 or 2019 might break. If this happens, SAP will not be able to provide any support for Office 365 in relation to BEx tools

Are there any plans to support BEx Analyzer and BEx workbooks beyond October 2025, especially for customers planning their BW strategy over the next several years with SAP BW 7.5 PCE?

  • There is No long-term support of BEx Analyzer, Workbooks and BEx Broadcasting (Pre-calculation). SAP can only support such versions of third-party products (MS Office) for which the third-party vendor (Microsoft) still provides maintenance, and in the case of excel dependent BEx tools, the last supported Office versions are Office 2016 and Office 2019.
  • No support offered beyond October 14, 2025 for Excel dependent BEx tools.

Will SAP continue to release SAP GUI updates (compatible with BEx Analyzer) for the full NetWeaver 7.5 maintenance window (through end of 2027 or 2030 extended)?

  • SAP NetWeaver 7.5 will be supported until the end of 2027, with optional extended maintenance until the end of 2030.
  • SAP will continue to ship the BEx FEPs that are compatible with SAP GUI, but these will only contain the BEx tools that do not have a dependency with MS Office. i.e., after October 14th, these will no longer contain the installation files for BEx Analyzer and BEx Broadcasting (Pre-calculation). This means, installing the new FEP will remove any existing installation of the excel based tools.
  • While it may still be technically possible to use BEx Analyzer and Pre-calculation server if the old installation files were preserved, this will no longer qualify as a standard installation of BEx Tools and SAP cannot guarantee that it will work without issues.

Conversion Questions:

When we convert a report from BEx to AFO, does the output stay in the same format (ie same data in same columns)?

  • Mostly Yes, the table structure and measure/dimensions are replicated like in the source system, which includes respecting common filtering conditions.
    • Underlying query logic is preserved when opening BEx workbooks in AFO.
    • However, UI rendering, formatting, and charting might look different as AFO uses Excel's engine, so custom formatting from BEx might need manual tweaks.

Is there any limitation on the data AFO can access/any reason to think that if we have something in a BEx report we can’t get it in an AFO report?

  • No, additional BW authorizations should be required to refresh a BEx Query in Analysis for Office compared to BEx Analyzer.

How do users access AFO? Does it depend/matter which SAP GUI users are on?

  • Analysis for Office is a client tool which will need to be installed on the end user’s machines. There is no dependency on SAP GUI. Refer to https://support.sap.com/content/dam/launchpad/en_us/pam/pam-essentials/AO_28_PAM_Additional_Info.pdf for more details

What are the limitations of conversion of BEx to AfO Workbooks using Mass Conversion tool?

  • The following features are not automatically converted but required some adjustment: Styles​, Conditions, Exceptions​, VBA API​, Navigation Panes, Dropdown Boxes, Button​s
  • The workbooks with given features can be identified during the Pre-Assessment

How can we ensure functional parity when replacing BEx queries with modern tools?

  • SAP reporting tools such as Analysis for Office, Business Objects Business Intelligence, and SAP Analytics Cloud do not replace the need for BEx Queries. Instead, BEx Queries are consumed by these solutions to query the data.

For conversion effort, let say few thousand of server workbooks were saved in the BW. Is there a way to assess which are effectively in use?

  • Yes, Pre-assessment service from SAP will identify the Workbooks (saved on BW server) which are frequently used. Local workbooks saved on user desktop can not be identified as part of this assessment.

License:

What are the licensing implications when moving from BEx to new tools like SAP Analysis for Office or SAP Analytics Cloud?

  • SAP Analysis for Office and SAP Analytics Cloud required additional licensing which is not provided with an SAP BW license. Please discuss the options with your Account Executive.

Is license for Analysis for office based on number of users?

  • Analysis for Office, Standard Edition is available to use if you have a BusinessObjects license.
  • Analysis for Office, SAC Edition is available if you have an SAC subscription. The SAC Edition does require you to have an SAC user, so it is effectively user-based licensing.

Do customer need to buy additional license if they already have BOBJ license?

  • No, every BOBJ license also includes Analysis for Office.

Planning and Next Steps:

What are the first steps we should take to prepare for the BEx end of support?

  • Assess your current BEx landscape: Identify all BEx reports, their usage, and dependencies.
  • Educate stakeholders: Inform relevant business and IT teams about the upcoming changes and their implications.
  • Evaluate replacement tools: Understand the features and capabilities of SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office and SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Develop a migration plan: Outline timelines, resources, and a phased approach if necessary.

Who should be involved in planning and executing the migration from BEx?

  • A cross-functional team is ideal, including IT (SAP BW/BI specialists), business users from various departments who rely on BEx reports, and may be project management.

What kind of training will our users and developers need for the new tools?

  • Users will need training on how to use SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office or SAP Analytics Cloud for report consumption, ad-hoc analysis, and potentially report creation.
  • Developers will need training on the new development environments and best practices for the chosen replacement tools

Conclusion:

We have made every effort to cover the important FAQs, however we understand you may have additional questions. Please refer to standard SAP guides or Feel free to use the comments section to ask them. We are committed to keeping this blog up-to-date with the latest information. We hope this has been a helpful resource.

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere is coming! Controlled release i
entry.content:
Hi @Max_Gander we have been doing some development in a project using seamless planning for a while now, however, I can't find the information regarding the availability of hierarchy (from a public dimension) in Datasphere. I understand that we can see dimension table and their text being exposed to Datasphere space, but how about hierarchy (table)?

Our objective is to combine (union) several planning models in Datasphere - just like the composite provider in SAP BW, and eventually make an analytic model on top of it. However, I can't find a way to associate the hierarchy from public dimension since none are available. I can see that this seems to be a very common practice for a lot of customers. Do you know anything about this?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Unable to Clear Payments Due as Invoice Amount Appears Zero
entry.content:
There are open invoices and payments in the customer account monitor. When a payment is selected and clicked on 'Clear Manually', the payment cannot be matched with the invoices as their amounts appear as zero.

Why does this issue occur?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Clear Payments Due as Invoice Amount Appear...
entry.content:
The issue occurs because the invoice is in one currency (e.g., USD) and the payment is in another (e.g., EUR), but the required exchange rates are not maintained in the system.

Please ensure that exchange rates between USD and EUR (or vice versa) are properly maintained.

entry.title: Re: Display Icon in object header
entry.content:
Hi @VISHAL_JHA 

Object page header expects a property name (field name) or icon name, not a full URL or sap-icon://.

Instead try using 

UI.headerInfo.typeImageUrlStringThis annotation contains the URL of an image representing an entity.

If you still want to use imageUrl which is "This annotation represents a path to an element containing the URL of an image representing the entity instance" then First initialize data(icon) = 'sap-icon://accelerated' and then imageUrl="icon" within the object page header.

Details are given in this documentation -https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw75/7.5.5/en-US/f8/af07bb0770414bb38a25cae29a12e9/content.htm?no_cache=true within in the "UI.headerInfo" section

entry.title: Re: Error note in campaign execution for generating expor...
entry.content:
@valeskaibscher smartsquarehmh wrote:

Hi everyone,

we ran a campaign in which an export file should have been generated. The campaign is running on error with this note CUAN_MKT_EXEC_FRW059 

Does anyone know what it means?

Best regards,

Valeska

Hi Valeska,

The CUAN_MKT_EXEC_FRW059 error in SAP Marketing Cloud usually means your campaign failed to generate an export file. This often happens due to permission issues (the system can't write the file), an incorrect file path/name, or problems with the data being exported. To fix it, first, double-check your campaign's export settings and review the system logs for more detailed error messages.

entry.title: Adding custom events in Business Network for Logistics - Global Track and Trace(GTT)
entry.content:
Businesses often need to track internal or external events that are specific to their industry or driven by internal KPIs to monitor the status of business documents. SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace(BN4L GTT) offers public APIs that support many common, industry-standard event types. Additionally, customers can use the "Other" event type to track events that fall outside predefined event types.

However, when even that is not sufficient, SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace(BN4L GTT) provides a flexible framework to define and integrate custom event, allowing customers to capture and monitor events that are uniquely relevant to their business processes.

In this blog, I will share how easily the customers can create their own custom events via the standard apps available.

 

Prerequisites:

  • The SAP Business Network for Logistics (BN4L) sample extractor code, delivered by the product team, should be implemented in the SAP S/4HANA system to enable the integration of event data with GTT.
  • To configure and add custom events in SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace (GTT), the IT user or system integrator must have the DataModelAdminTemplate role assigned in the SAP BTP subaccount where the GTT application is subscribed.

 

Overview of Steps:

  • Step 1: Create and Extend the Custom Event in S/4HANA TM
    • Define the custom event in the S/4HANA Transportation Management (TM) system and enhance the BN4L GTT extractor logic to send the planned date and time of the event to GTT.
  • Step 2: Configure the Custom Event in BN4L GTT
    • Use the SAP Business Network GTT app to configure the custom event and map it to the corresponding IDoc segmentreceived from the source system.

Detailed Steps: 

Step 1: Create and Extend the Custom Event in S/4Hana TM

  • Step 1.1: Configuration of new event type in S/4Hana TM
    • We begin by setting up the new custom event in the S/4HANA Transportation Management (TM) system. To do this, navigate to the SAP Customizing Implementation Guide (SPRO) and follow the path: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide(IMG) -> Transportation Management -> Integration -> Tracking and Tracing of Processes and Documents -> Define Transportation Activities for Tracking and Tracing. Here, you can define the new event type that will later be mapped and sent to SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace (GTT).Note: This configuration path is based on S/4HANA 2022 release. It may vary slightly in earlier or more recent versions of Transportation Management.
  • Step 1.2: Extending the GTT extractor code 
    • Edit the class "ZCL_GTT_STS_PE_FO_FILLER" and add a new method called ZZ_CUSTOMSHIP_EVENT to populate the planned date and time of the custom event "ZZ_CUSTOM_EVENT" into the IDoc. Please note that the actual ABAP class to be modified may vary depending on your tracking configuration set up in transaction "ZGTT_TRACK_CONF". This is because the data filler class responsible for planned events is determined by the specific tracking configuration defined for your scenario.

      In this example, I am sending the departure date and time of the source location as the planned timestamp for the custom event. However, you can implement your own logic to calculate the appropriate date and time based on other available fields from the Freight Order, and then include that information in the IDoc accordingly.

    • Method Declaration and Code Snippet: 

 methods ZZ_CUSTOMSHIP_EVENT
    importing
      !IV_TOR_ID type /SCMTMS/TOR_ID optional
      !IT_STOP type /SCMTMS/T_EM_BO_TOR_STOP optional
      !IT_LOC_ADDR type /BOFU/T_ADDR_POSTAL_ADDRESSK
      !IT_STOP_POINTS type ZIF_GTT_STS_EF_TYPES=>TT_STOP_POINTS optional
      !IS_APP_OBJECTS type TRXAS_APPOBJ_CTAB_WA
    changing
      !CT_EXPEVENTDATA type ZIF_GTT_STS_EF_TYPES=>TT_EXPEVENTDATA .
METHOD ZZ_CUSTOMSHIP_EVENT.
    DATA: ls_loc_addr TYPE REF TO /bofu/s_addr_postal_addressk,
          lt_loc_root TYPE /scmtms/t_bo_loc_root_k.

    FIELD-SYMBOLS <ls_root> TYPE /scmtms/s_em_bo_tor_root.

    ASSIGN is_app_objects-maintabref->* TO <ls_root>.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT it_stop USING KEY parent_seqnum ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_stop>) WHERE parent_node_id = <ls_root>-node_id.
      CHECK <ls_stop>-stop_cat = /scmtms/if_common_c=>c_stop_category-outbound AND
            <ls_stop>-plan_trans_time IS NOT INITIAL.

      READ TABLE it_stop_points REFERENCE INTO DATA(ls_stop_points)
                                     WITH KEY log_locid = <ls_stop>-log_locid
                                              seq_num   = <ls_stop>-seq_num.
      CHECK sy-subrc = 0.

*      TEST-SEAM shp_dep_lt_loc_root.
        /scmtms/cl_pln_bo_data=>get_loc_data(
          EXPORTING
            it_key      = VALUE #( ( key = <ls_stop>-log_loc_uuid ) )
          CHANGING
            ct_loc_root = lt_loc_root ).
*      END-TEST-SEAM.
      READ TABLE lt_loc_root REFERENCE INTO DATA(ls_loc_root)
                                     WITH KEY key COMPONENTS key = <ls_stop>-log_loc_uuid.
      CHECK sy-subrc = 0.

      DATA(lv_tz) = COND tznzone( WHEN ls_loc_root->time_zone_code IS NOT INITIAL
                                  THEN ls_loc_root->time_zone_code
                                  ELSE sy-zonlo ).

      DATA(lv_exp_datetime) = <ls_stop>-plan_trans_time.

      zcl_gtt_sts_tools=>convert_utc_timestamp(
        EXPORTING
          iv_timezone  =  lv_tz
        CHANGING
          cv_timestamp = lv_exp_datetime ).

      mv_milestonecnt += 1.
      APPEND VALUE #(
      appsys            = mo_ef_parameters->get_appsys(  )
      appobjtype        = mo_ef_parameters->get_app_obj_types( )-aotype
      language          = sy-langu
      appobjid          = is_app_objects-appobjid
      milestone         = zif_gtt_sts_constants=>cs_milestone-fo_cust_event
      evt_exp_datetime  = |0{ lv_exp_datetime }|
      evt_exp_tzone     = lv_tz
      locid1            = <ls_stop>-log_locid
      locid2            = ls_stop_points->stop_id
      loctype           = zcl_gtt_sts_tools=>get_location_type( iv_locno = <ls_stop>-log_locid ) ) TO ct_expeventdata.

      CLEAR: lt_loc_root, ls_loc_root.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.​

By implementing the above method in the class ZCL_GTT_STS_PE_FO_FILLER, you complete the process of sending the new custom event to BN4L GTT via the IDoc.

Step 2: Configure the Custom Event in BN4L GTT

The first step is to add the custom event in the relevant tracked process. A tracked process in SAP Global Track and Trace (GTT) represents a business process that involves multiple parties and may also include other interrelated processes. It defines a sequence of planned events—referred to as milestones in systems like SAP Event Management—that are expected to occur during process execution. As the process unfolds, GTT captures actual events, which can either be the realization of planned events or unexpected/unplanned occurrences, providing real-time visibility and actionable insights.

A common example of a standard tracked process is a shipment, and this blog will focus primarily on how to enhance and customize event tracking within a shipment tracked process.

  • Step 2.1: Create a new custom event in "Manage Model" app of SAP BN4L 
    • Login to BN4L - GTT app and navigate to 'Manage Model' app under 'Model Management' section. In this step, we will extend the standard delivered model 'gttft1 - GTT Standard Model' to add the custom event. You can click on standard model and go to edit mode to add the new event.
    • Navigate to 'Event Type Pool' tab where all the standard and user-defined events are maintained.
    • Click on 'Create' button on the top of the list of events and provide the information in the pop-up 'Create Event Type' for the new custom event such as name, description and the tracked process it will be associated to. As I am adding the custom event for the 'Shipment' tracked process, I have selected the 'Shipment' from the drop-down field:
    • (Optional): Once the new event is created, it will appear in the list of events on the left side of the section. You will then have the option to add any user-model fields that are specifically relevant to this new event. By default, the new event inherits all the standard core-model fields under the section CoreModel.Event. However, if you have a business requirement to capture any additional specific fields or data for the custom event, you can add them here under the User Model Fields section.
  • Step 2.2: Maintain the required configuration for custom event 

    In this step, I will configure the newly created event by classifying it as either an Admissible Planned or Admissible Unplanned event for a tracked process. This classification should be based on your specific business requirements.

    For this example, I will configure the custom event as a Planned event for the 'Shipment' tracked process.

    • Navigate to 'Tracked Process' tab and scroll to 'Shipment' tracked process from the list of tracked processes.
    • Select 'Admissible Planned Event' from 'Content' drop-down as we are adding the custom event as planned event. You will see all the User Model Planned Event types and the Standard Model Event Types which are relevant for 'Shipment' Tracked Process.
    • Click on 'Add' button and add the required configuration for the new event. Once added, click on 'Save' button to save the changes related to custom event. 
      • Tolerances: The tolerance value is used to calculate the earliest and latest timestamps of the events if it's not provided by the source of the planned event.
      • Periodic Overdue Detection:It's used to define how often you want the system to check the status of overdue events. In the below screenshot, I have defined it as 10 minutes and the system will check the status of overdue events every 10 minutes. You can also define how many times the system should check the Overdue status by maintaining the value for the field 'Max Overdue Detection'. 
      • You can also configure notification emails to be sent if an event becomes overdue and has not yet been reported. This helps alert the relevant stakeholders so they can take timely action.
    • Step 2.3: Map the event with the IDOC segment

      In this step, we will map the newly created custom event for the Shipment tracked process to the corresponding IDoc segment in the incoming shipment data—either from an external system or from SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management. (Extending the S/4Hana TM extractor code for sending the data for custom events will be covered in another blog)

      Since the custom event has been added as an Admissible Planned Event, the planned timestamps will be provided by the source system through the shipment document.

    • Navigate to 'IDOC Integration' tab and select 'Shipment' in the tracked process field. This will list all the data (events and fields) related to shipment tracked process and how it is mapped to the standard delivered IDOC for shipment creation.
    • Check the newly created custom event under the 'Tracked Process/Events' section and map it to the event name 'ZZ_CUSTOM_EVENT' created in S/4Hana Transportation Management in Step 1.

       

    • Click ‘Deploy’ to save and deploy the model. Once deployment is successful—usually within a few seconds—the model status will change to Active.

This will complete the configuration required on BN4L GTT to add any custom event and map it to the incoming IDOC from an external system or SAP S/4Hana Transporation Management. 

Testing Results:

The new custom event will appear in the Track Shipment app for shipments created after the implementation is completed. Since the event was configured as a Planned Event under the Shipment Tracked Process, it will be visible in the Tracking Timeline of the shipment.

You can also test the setup by manually reporting the event via the same app using the “Report” button, which allows you to simulate and validate the event flow.

 

In my upcoming blog, I will explain how to recieve the actual event from an external system or a third-party systems using the SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace API's.

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Why GRC Matters for Growing Businesses
entry.content:
“Governance, Risk, and Compliance is not for us, we’re a mid-size company and these topics are only for large and international groups”. if I was given a penny every time someone said this, then I would be a millionaire! And much like Jason and his Golden Fleece it is a myth.

Companies of all sizes face risks, and companies of all sizes must comply with regulatory frameworks.

But a risk always has two facets: the potential negative outcome of course, and the potential upside.

In this blog, I therefore decided to look at both and highlight how GRC can help mid-size companies achieve their objectives and thrive in a complex business landscape.

 

Main Priorities for Small and Midsize Businesses

 

To be able to identify how GRC can be a value-add activity, the first order of business is to understand main priorities for executives in these companies.

Based on 40+ studies and reports from various regions, a summary picture is as follows:

As the saying goes: clear as mud, right? So, let’s apply some analytics to this picture and group the priorities alongside 2 buckets: top challenges that SMEs need to address and top opportunities that they can leverage.

 

Focus on top 5 challenges for SMBs

 

 

Governance, Risk, and Compliance is not a magic wand, so won’t solve all the issues of course. But it can help mitigate some of these challenges:

 

Digitization and Technology

 

 

Digital transformation: by “safeguarding” the digitalised landscape with embedded IT application controls, and identity and access management to ensure compliant access
Online presence: by protecting sensitive data from misuse and malicious access

Poor use of technology: by detecting and deterring cyber-attacks, but also by enforcing data sovereignty and residency requirements

 

Funding

 

Funding the business: by implementing IPO requirements

Seeking funding from investors: by providing investors assurance on financial reports

 

Rising (operating) Costs

 

Increase in ongoing costs: by improving operational efficiency (e.g. optimal operating cycle), and identifying invoice & inventory discrepancies

Managing taxes (incl. filing): by automating tax reporting and filing

Tracking expenses: by identifying payment anomalies (e.g. duplicate claims & other fraud, expense claims outside of policy)

 

Sales and Revenue

 

Attracting new customers: by screening potential customers effortlessly to ensure that no business is performed with sanctioned parties

Client dependence: by risk assessing customers to reduce dependencies and improve reliability

Converting leads: by protecting sensitive customer data

 

Talent Shortage and Staffing

Recruiting talented staff: by ensuring that localization of contracts is correctly performed for onboarding

Churn and instability: by promoting (and enforcing) ethical behaviour

Retaining employees: with ease of onboarding users to core systems and safe remote working

Employee productivity: with rapid and compliant user access provisioning, and by providing self-service password and access request

Remote workforce: by simplifying and securing logon to source systems

 

Focus on top 5 opportunities

 

But it’s not all about managing the lurking threats. As all successful businesses know, it’s about leveraging the opportunities when they present themselves, and enhancing chances of success.

Let’s change our lens and focus on these upsides instead:

 Here again, to be able to provide actionable suggestions, let’s break it down:

 

Digitization and Technology

Improving online presence:  by implementing data sovereignty and residency requirements, detecting and deterring cyber-attacks, and ultimately protecting sensitive data from misuse and malicious access

 

Key Talent Staffing

 

Attracting key talent but also Diversity, equity and inclusion: with evidence of ethical behaviour and diversity, and no greenwashing especially when it comes to child or forced labour

Personalized HR outreach: with relevant localization of contracts to ensure applicability in relevant geographies

 

 

Reducing (operating) Costs

Cutting costs: by proactively detecting payment anomalies (duplicate invoices or fraudulent activities)

 

Sales and Revenue

 

Create new revenue streams: by screening potential customers effortlessly to avoid high-risk businesses, individuals, and entities and more quickly confirming business parties

Converting leads to customers: by protecting sensitive customer data so protecting the company’s reputation

 

Scaling and Expanding

Relocate or enter new markets: by screening business partners to avoid high-risk businesses, individuals, and entities and more quickly confirming business parties

Expand internationally: by automating tasks for international trade management and reacting more rapidly to changing trade dynamics (including tariff changes)

 

Why now?

 

These challenges and opportunities of course aren’t exclusively relevant to small and medium organizations. But their top ranking reflects what business owners have rated their most pressing matters. Nevertheless, most companies would have very similar topics on their management agenda and GRC can act as a business enabler – not just a compliance exercise:

 

Digitization and Technology

Funding

Operating Costs

Sales and Revenue

Talent and Staffing

Scaling and Expanding

•   Digitized & integrated controls & security checks, and identity management

•   Protect sensitive data from misuse and malicious access

•   Detect and deter cyber-attack, enforce data sovereignty and residency requirements

•   Implement IPO compliance requirements

•   Provide investors assurance on financial reports

•   Improve operational efficiency, and identify invoice & inventory discrepancies

•   Automate tax reporting and filing

•   Identify payment anomalies

•   Screen potential customers effortlessly

•   Protect sensitive customer data

•   Protect brand with evidence of ethical behavior

•   Safe remote working

•   Rapid and compliant user access provisioning, self-service password and access request, and secure logon to multiple systems

•   Screen business partners to avoid high-risk businesses, individuals, and entities

•   Automate tasks for international trade management and compliance

And, even if there is no better time than the present, allow me to share a few insights from experts on why now is really a good time to start:

What about you, does this resonate with the top priorities for your company?

If you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: Re: Hi Team,Open GR/IR balance migration tools in S/4 Han...
entry.content:
below answer for your queries:

1. Split line item – two different PO?, if two different PO created need to inform to vendors all new PO’s.

Answer1: 1 PO with 2 line items, don't place it into 2 POs.

2. if GRN posted in SAP will trigger stock GL accounts and Gr/IR & Inspection lot also will generates, is there any way without impacting stock amount and entry in GR/IR GL.

Answer2: the PO history line item is not posted into stock GL account, you should consult with your finance consultant, she/he will give you specific GL.

3. If book the pending Invoice without GRN, GR/IR will not get cleared right?

Answer3: it will get cleared by manual, finance will migrate it in GR/IR balance and take notes for this document.

any idea about SAP standard solution OMQ2 (program RM06EEI1) to update PO history in S/4 HANA.

Normally, we can take migration cockpit solution. If you don't want to use DMC, you can use LSMW, this program RM06EEI1 also exists in LSMW standard input program, and in LSMW, it is more agile, you can also use IDOC or BAPI program, and you also have the load template with fields visibility to input your data value.

2. Import your SAP UI5 form in SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Use of MRP-Relevant Dep. Requirements in MRP 4 view
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I have a specific requirement and would appreciate any assistance.

I intend to execute the Material Requirements Planning (MRP) for the header material (Finished Goods - FG) by maintaining the demand in transaction code MD61. The FG consists of components—one Semi-Finished Good (SFG) and one Raw Material (ROH). My objective is to run MRP in such a way that the components are excluded from the planning process, meaning that dependent requirements for the FG should not generate plans for its components.

Based on information available in the (SCN), it is suggested that setting the MRP-Relevant Dependent Requirements field in the MRP 4 view of the FG material to 1 prevents the system from generating plans for the components. However, despite implementing this setting, the planning is still generated.

When I set the MRP-Relevant Dependent Requirements field to 1 for the components as well, the plan is not generated.

I am looking for an approach where, for a particular FG, its components should be planned, while for the same components used in another FG, the plan should not be generated.

Is there a specific setting that needs to be configured in the material master of the header to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: 自動付款可否跨公司不同公司幣別進行付款
entry.content:
Hello @jakeyin1 ,

This is a standard system behaviour that the sending and paying company codes must be in the same country  and have the same currencies. Please refer to KBA: 2257645 - Error F3063 when doing customizing in transaction FBZP - SAP for Me

If the sending and paying company codes are in different countries and currencies, please consider to use the

In-House Banking (6BP) process: Process Navigator - SAP for Me

In-house banking offers bank accounts for all subsidiaries in different account currencies. Subsidiaries send their payment files using standard payment processes offered within financial accounting. In-house banking sends electronic bank statements back to clear the open items accordingly.

In-house banking supports payments between subsidiaries (intercompany payments), payments towards external business partners (payments on behalf), and central incoming payments (for example, resulting from external zero balancing postings).

Kind Regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP SF EC WF Email Notifications
entry.content: Hi, Please create a workflow email configuration and assign it to the workflow. Please refer to help document: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/implementing-and-managing-workflows/creating-workflow-email-configuration
